<!-- Generated by scripts/sync_chebfun_examples.py. -->
<!-- Source: https://www.chebfun.org/examples/approx/Galleries.html -->

<div class="chebfun-import chebfun-example-import">
<div class='page-header'>
<h1>Gallery and Gallerytrig</h1>
<h2>Hrothgar and Nick Trefethen, December 2014 in <a href='../'>approx</a><span><a href='/examples/approx/Galleries.m'>download</a><span class='sep-sm'>&middot;</span><a href='//github.com/chebfun/examples/blob/master/approx/Galleries.m'>view on GitHub</a></span></h2>
</div>

<div id='content' class="col-sm-12 example" role="main">
<p>For many years MATLAB has had a <code>gallery</code> command to construct interesting matrices.  Beginning with the release of Version 5.1 this month, Chebfun has a gallery command too, which constructs interesting functions, i.e., chebfuns. To see what's available, type <code>help cheb/gallery</code>.</p>
<p>Here is a particularly attractive image.  The command</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">f = cheb.gallery('rose');</pre>

<p>produces a complex chebfun <code>f</code> that can be visualized strikingly with <code>fill</code>:</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">fill(real(f),imag(f),[1 0 0]), axis equal off</pre>

<p><img src="../../../images/approx/Galleries_01.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p>
<p>Some gallery examples produce results so simple that a line of obvious Chebfun code would have had the same effect:</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">plot(cheb.gallery('airy')), ylim([-.6 .6]), title('Airy function')</pre>

<p><img src="../../../images/approx/Galleries_02.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p>
<p>(This is essentially just <code>chebfun(@airy, [-40,40])</code>.) Sometimes however you may know what the function looks like but need a reminder of how to generate it.  Here is a polynomial of degree 10,000:</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">plot(cheb.gallery('zigzag'),'m'), ylim([-.13 .09])
title('polynomial of degree 10,000')</pre>

<p><img src="../../../images/approx/Galleries_03.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p>
<p>The gallery also includes a familiar Chebfun motto,</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">plot(cheb.gallery('motto'),'k','linewidth',1.6)
axis equal off</pre>

<p><img src="../../../images/approx/Galleries_04.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p>
<p>For periodic functions, there is also <code>cheb.gallerytrig</code>. (Two-dimensional functions and <code>cheb.gallery2</code> are discussed in a separate Example.) For example, here is an approximation to a fractal function of Weierstrass,</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">plot(cheb.gallerytrig('weierstrass'),'linewidth',1,'color',[0 .6 0])</pre>

<p><img src="../../../images/approx/Galleries_05.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p>
<p>Here is a periodic example whose function is generated by solving the periodic differential equation</p>
<p>$$ u'' + u' + 600(1+\sin x) u = 1, \qquad x\in[-\pi,\pi] . $$</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">plot(cheb.gallerytrig('tsunami'),'color',[.8 .5 0]), ylim([-.2,.2])</pre>

<p><img src="../../../images/approx/Galleries_06.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p>
<p>To find details of how a gallery example is constructed, try e.g. <code>type cheb/gallery</code> or <code>edit cheb/gallery</code>.  We are always glad to see interesting new examples: if you have something to suggest, please get in touch!</p></div>
        </div>
    </div>
</div>
